zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Beaumont於1320年出生在英國時，她的父親Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris50歲，母親Alice Comyn31歲。她於大約1337年與Henry of Grosmont 1st Duke of Lancaster在英國藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。她去世於1361年3月24日之後在她的家鄉，埋葬在英國萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel de Beaumont于1320年出生在英国时，她的父亲Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris50岁，母亲Alice Comyn31岁。她于大约1337年与Henry of Grosmont 1st Duke of Lancaster在英国藍卡爾结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。她去世于1361年3月24日之后在她的家乡，埋葬在英国萊希斯特斯爾萊希斯特。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
English (Kent): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Siegmund.
German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: English (mainly Lancashire): variant of   Beaumont  .
Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobel
meanings: Variant spelling of   Isabel .

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Beaumont was born in 1320, in England, her father, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, was 50 and her mother, Alice Comyn, was 31. She married Henry of Grosmont 1st Duke of Lancaster about 1337, in Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. She died after 24 March 1361, in her hometown, and was buried in Leicester, Leicestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Beaumont est née en 1320, à Angleterre, son père, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, avait 50 ans et sa mère, Alice Comyn, avait 31 ans. Elle a épousé Henry of Grosmont 1st Duke of Lancaster environ 1337, à Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Elle est décédée après 24 mars 1361, dans sa ville natale, et a été enterrée à Leicester, Leicestershire, Angleterre.

Sie hat um 1337 in Lancashire, England Henry of Grosmont 1st Duke of Lancaster geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Sie starb nach 24. März 1361 in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Leicester, Leicestershire, England begraben.

Lei sposato Henry of Grosmont 1st Duke of Lancaster circa 1337, nel Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì dopo il 24 marzo 1361, nel sua cittadina, e fu sepolta a Leicester, Leicestershire, England.

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Beaumont(이)가 1320년 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris(은)는 50세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Comyn(은)는 31세였다. 그녀는 대략 1337년 영국 랭카실르에서 Henry of Grosmont 1st Duke of Lancaster(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1361년 3월 24일 이후 그녀의 고향에서 사망했으며 영국 레이스스터실르 레이스스터에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Beaumont nasceu em 1320, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris, tinha 50 anos e sua mãe, Alice Comyn, tinha 31 anos. Ela casou-se com Henry of Grosmont 1st Duke of Lancaster aproximadamente 1337, em Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ela faleceu após 24 de março de 1361, em sua cidade natal, e foi sepultada em Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.Leicester Castle, Leicestershire, Englan: лисестир т кастл вию, лисестиршир, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Beaumont родилась в 1320 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Henry de Beaumont 4th Earl of Buchan Jure Uxoris было 50 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Comyn, 31. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Henry of Grosmont 1st Duke of Lancaster около 1337 г. в местности ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Она умерла после 24 марта 1361 г. в её родном городе и была похоронена в местности лисестир, лисестиршир, Англия.

Se casó con Henry of Grosmont 1st Duke of Lancaster aproximadamente 1337, en Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió después de 24 de marzo de 1361, en su pueblo, y fue sepultada en Leicester, Leicestershire, Inglaterra.
